evals.prompt: I just closed a bunch of bd issues this sprint and merged 3 PRs. The README.md is getting long — can you sync the docs and make sure everything's in order? Use sync-docs.
evals.expected_output: Runs context_gatherer.py and doc_structure_analyzer.py, reports what was found (closed issues, PRs, any drift), identifies README extraction candidates or MISSING docs/ files, and either fixes them or gives a clear plan with next steps.

assertions.description: Ran context_gatherer.py and reported bd closed issues or merged PRs from the output
assertions.check: result.md mentions context_gatherer or bd closed issues or merged PRs with specific data

assertions.description: Ran doc_structure_analyzer.py and used its output to identify doc issues
assertions.check: result.md references MISSING, STALE, EXTRACTABLE, or BLOATED status from the analyzer

assertions.description: Produced at least one concrete recommendation or action (not just a vague summary)
assertions.check: result.md names a specific file (e.g. docs/hooks.md) or section with a specific next step

assertions.description: Used the skill scripts rather than just reading files manually
assertions.check: result.md shows script execution output, not just manual file reading

evals.prompt: Run sync-docs --fix on this project and remember what you did with bd.
evals.expected_output: Runs doc_structure_analyzer.py --fix --bd-remember, creates scaffold files for any missing docs/ subsystems, persists a bd memory with the summary, then validates the created files with validate_doc.py.

assertions.check: result.md shows the --fix command was executed

assertions.description: Ran with --bd-remember or manually ran bd remember with a summary
assertions.check: result.md shows bd remember was called and reports the memory key

assertions.description: At least one scaffold file was created in docs/
assertions.check: result.md lists a docs/*.md file created, OR reports no gaps found (valid outcome)

assertions.description: Ran validate_doc.py on created files to confirm schema
assertions.check: result.md shows validate_doc.py was run and reports pass/fail for created files

evals.prompt: Do a doc audit. I think the README has sections that should be in docs/ but I'm not sure which ones.
evals.expected_output: Runs the full 5-phase sync-docs workflow: gathers context, runs drift detection, runs doc_structure_analyzer.py, and identifies EXTRACTABLE/BLOATED sections with specific suggestions for what goes in which docs/ file before recommending or making changes.

assertions.description: Ran doc_structure_analyzer.py and referenced its structured output
assertions.check: result.md cites the analyzer output (EXTRACTABLE, BLOATED, line count, or specific section names from the report)

assertions.description: Named specific README sections with their suggested docs/ destination
assertions.check: result.md lists at least 2 specific sections (e.g. '## Policy System → docs/policies.md') not just generic advice

assertions.description: Report is actionable — tells user exactly what to do next, not just observations
assertions.check: result.md includes a prioritized list or clear next steps, not just 'the README could be shorter'

assertions.description: Did not edit or create any files (audit only)
assertions.check: result.md does not claim to have modified README.md or created docs/ files
